1989_03_29-India-Mumbai-ISKCON_Chowpatty-Unfortunates_Are_Fortunates-SB-7_15_68_st02_vr01

HH Radhanath Swami

Meta Data v8

Event Name/Title

Bhagwatam Class / Unfortunates Are Fortunates

Event Date

29 Mar 1989

Event Location

Mumbai

Event Type

Temple - SB class

Transcriber

Sanatan Gopal Das

Completed

02 Aug 2021

Source Audio

https://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-004-Unfortunates%20Are%20Fortunates%20(Mumbai)-SB_7_15_68-dt_89_03_29.mp3

Source Video

NA

Verse

SB 7.15.68

Tags – Places

New Vrindan

Tags – Pastimes

Story of Pandavas, Story of Prahlad Maharaj

Tags – Persons

Krishna, Pandavas, Prahlad Maharaj, Narad Muni, Yudhisthir, Arjun

Tags – Themes

Faith in Krishna, Krishna protects His devotees, Taking shelter of Krishna, Soul, Krishna hears everything you desire

Lecture Transcription

yathā hi yūyaṁ nṛpa-deva dustyajād

āpad-gaṇād uttaratātmanaḥ prabhoḥ

yat-pāda-paṅkeruha-sevayā bhavān

ahāraṣīn nirjita-dig-gajaḥ kratūn

[Sb 7.15.68]

Translation

O King Yudhiṣṭhira, because of your service to the Supreme Lord, all of you Pāṇḍavas defeated the greatest dangers posed by numerous kings and demigods. By serving the lotus feet of Kṛṣṇa, you conquered great enemies, who were like elephants, and thus you collected ingredients for sacrifice. By His grace, may you be delivered from material involvement.

Purport

Placing himself as an ordinary householder, Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira inquired from Nārada Muni how a gṛha-mūḍha-dhī, a person who is entangled in household life and who thus continues to remain a fool, can be delivered. Nārada Muni encouraged Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira by saying, “You are already on the safe side because you, along with your entire family, have become a pure devotee of Kṛṣṇa.” By Kṛṣṇa’s grace, the Pāṇḍavas conquered in the Battle of Kurukṣetra and were saved from many dangers posed not only by kings but sometimes even by the demigods. Thus they are a practical example of how to live in security and safety by the grace of Kṛṣṇa. Everyone should follow the example of the Pāṇḍavas, who showed how to be saved by the grace of Kṛṣṇa. Our Kṛṣṇa consciousness movement is intended to teach how everyone can live peacefully in this material world and at the end of life return home, back to Godhead. In the material world there are always dangers at every step (padaṁ padaṁ yad vipadāṁ na teṣām). Nonetheless, if one takes shelter of Kṛṣṇa without hesitation and keeps under the shelter of Kṛṣṇa, he can easily cross the ocean of nescience. Samāśritā ye pada-pallava-plavaṁ mahat-padaṁ puṇya-yaśo murāreḥ. To the devotee, this great ocean of nescience becomes like a puddle of water in the hoofprint of a cow. A pure devotee, without embarrassing himself by trying for elevation in so many ways, stays in the safest position as a servant of Kṛṣṇa, and thus his life is eternally safe without a doubt.

Pandavas are most fortunate

The Pandavas are considered by Narada Muni to be the most fortunate. Great souls like Narada Muni do not consider the material circumstances, but they consider the consciousness. At this time the Pandavas having just completed a great Rajasuya sacrifice to conquer the entire world. Previously they were without anything, they were being unnecessarily discredited, they had lost their right to the kingdom, they had lost all material possessions. At one time the Pandavas were living in the forest wandering from place to place with not enough property to call their own to put the head of a pin through. Not only that but they were being their lives were being hunted by the Kurus and they were constantly subjected to the dangers of great rakshasas living in the jungles. At another time the Pandavas were living as the Supreme Kings of the entire Earth in their palace in Hastinapur. Now as far as Srila Narada Muni is concerned, the Pandavas are equally fortunate in both conditions.

Taking shelter of Krishna

Narada Muni sees the Pandavas as the most fortunate men on earth not because they're living in the jungle, not because they are the kings of Earth. For a great soul like Narada Muni it makes no difference whether you own the earth and you are the ruler of all and everyone is glorifying you or whether you have been unfairly deprived of everything in life, people only criticize you and you're living in the jungles with nothing except dangers all around. Narada Muni saw that the Pandavas were most fortunate because they were devoted to Krishna and one who is devoted to Krishna is protected on all sides by the Supreme Personality of Godhead. One may ask how can you say they were being protected when they lost everything. A devotee does not see protection in terms of material acquisitions or material loss. As long as one has faith, that faith in and of itself is the greatest wealth, the greatest acquisition in life and for one who is sincere, Krishna protects his faith. Krishna does not necessarily promise to protect your material body, in fact he promises that eventually he will not protect your material body. He promises that your material body whether you're a devotee or not we will all get diseased and die, there is no doubt. Dying today or dying tomorrow makes really very little difference. But it is the faith that we have in the mercy of God that is our real life. This physical body is not our life, this physical mind is not our life but our faith in Krishna, our faith in his orders, in our service to his devotees, that is our eternal life and Krishna for those who are sincere will always protect the devotee especially in this manner. Therefore, Narada Muni who was a great Acharya, who was well experienced in the ways of this world, he is praising the Pandavas because under all circumstances Krishna protected their devotion, He protected their faith.

Krishna protects His devotees

In fact, Prahlada Maharaja when he was being thrown off cliffs, thrown in boiling oil and being harassed by man-eating demons, he was considered by Narada Muni to be most fortunate and later on he was made the king of the rakshasas sitting on the throne of his father, he was also considered to be the most fortunate. So obviously Narada Muni’s idea of what his good fortune has nothing to do with whether you're on the top of the bottom, whether you're being honored or dishonored whether you're a success or a failure in this world whether you're so-called in comfort or whether you're in being tortured. Narada Muni’s standard of fortune is whether or not you are remembering Krishna with devotion. If you remember, if you are sincere and Krishna protects that devotion, He protects that faith then you are most fortunate. Krishna told Arjuna that you fight this battle you are most fortunate. If you lose, you die and you go to heaven. If you win, you rule over the earth. What is the difference? It makes no difference. Life or death, victory-defeat, success-failure makes no difference. The only thing that matters, ‘man-mana bhava mad-bhakto’, is that you are always thinking of Me, that you are My devotee, that you are worshiping Me and offering your homage unto Me. Krishna says in this way you will come to Me without fail, either in this life or in another life you will come to Me without fail. Prabhupada is quoting the famous verse from the Srimad-Bhagavatam,

samāśritā ye pada-pallava-plavaṁ

mahat-padaṁ puṇya-yaśo murāreḥ

bhavāmbudhir vatsa-padaṁ paraṁ padaṁ

padaṁ padaṁ yad vipadāṁ na teṣām

[SB 10.14.58]

[For those who have accepted the boat of the lotus feet of the Lord, who is the shelter of the cosmic manifestation and is famous as Murāri, the enemy of the Mura demon, the ocean of the material world is like the water contained in a calf’s hoof-print. Their goal is paraṁ padam, Vaikuṇṭha, the place where there are no material miseries, not the place where there is danger at every step.]

That this material world is a place where there is danger at every step. It is compared to a vast ocean, the ocean of birth and death and no mortal man on his own can cross the ocean. But for one who takes shelter of the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna, the ocean becomes reduced to the size of the amount of water contained in the footprint of the calf. In other words, when you become a devotee of the Lord the dangers of material existence no longer exist, but then how can we say this because we see that the devotees are generally put in more dangerous conditions than even those who do not take shelter of the lotus feet of Shri Krishna. So why is Krishna saying that there is no danger for one who takes shelter of Me? We see so many great souls, were tortured, were persecuted, were killed, were harassed, were deprived, who were very-very sincerely devoted to the Lord. Therefore, many people, they take to the worship of the demigods because they feel what is the question of being free from danger if you worship Krishna? Look at what happens to his devotees, if I worship the demigods nicely, I will get material benedictions, then I will be free from danger. But Krishna explains in the Gita, ‘Janma-mrtyu-jara-vyadhi duhkha-dosanudarsanam’. That birth, old age, disease and death are the real dangers, they are the real sources of misery and they are the real problems of life and no demigod can relieve you from birth, old age, disease and death for the body must be born and the body must die. That is the law of nature and that cannot be reversed. But if we take shelter of Lord Sri Krishna in his remembrance, we gradually become free from the bodily conception of life and when we become free from the bodily conception of life then there is no more danger.

Taking shelter of Krishna & Faith in Krishna

As long as you think you have this body, there is no possibility, it is impossible to be fearless, it is impossible to be immune from danger because the body is subject to danger at every step no matter who you are, no matter what you do. Therefore, one who takes shelter of Shri Krishna and identifies as the eternal servant of Krishna, does not identify as being the enjoyer of the field of this material body. Such a person is relieved of all dangers eternally. Why Krishna says in the Gita, the soul cannot be burned by fire, cannot be withered by the wind, cannot be cut into pieces by any weapon ‘na hanyate hanyamane sharire’. The soul is never slain. Even when the body is killed the soul remains as it is. Krishna says that some look upon the soul is amazing, some people speak of the soul is amazing. The soul is utterly amazing because it is completely beyond the limitations of this material reference of thinking and the activity of the soul is to always remember Krishna and to always be engaged in his loving service. So, one who takes shelter not of Krishna for material benediction, for one who takes shelter of Krishna identifying himself as the eternal servant of Krishna, such a person and such a person alone is free from all the dangers of material existence and such a person is most fortunate under any condition of life. You see, the great souls, they do not consider fortune, your material situation. Whatever situation you are in if it is helping you cry out for Krishna helplessly then you are most fortunate. In whatever material situation if it is causing you to become comfortable in this material world and with that comfortable situation you are not turning to Krishna for shelter, then you are most unfortunate.

Sometimes Krishna gives us what we want, sometimes he takes everything away, in fact Maharaja Yudhishthira at one time approached lord Krishna in this regard said Krishna, we are your devotee and you are the controller of everything. Not a blade of grass moves without your sanction, why is it that we have surrendered our lives to you and you have taken everything away, you have discredited us, you have disclaimed us, you have thrown us into the jungles and we're just wandering around like mendicants for fourteen years when we're supposed to be the kings of the earth not only that but our life is in constant danger and it's constantly inflicted with all sorts of inconveniences. Why you do like this to your devotee? Then Krishna's reply was ‘yasyāham anugṛhṇāmi
hariṣye tad-dhanaṁ śanaiḥ’ That when I show my kindness upon someone, I give that person whatever he wants. I make him famous, very popular, I make him very comfortable in his living conditions and I give him a very nice position, secure in this world, but when I the first instalment of my supreme mercy is, when I take everything away from my devotee, so that my devotee has nothing except Me to turn to.

Story of a devotee in New Vrindavan - Taking shelter of Krishna

There is a devotee in New Vrindavan this is just now, actually he came to live in Vrindavan in 1973 and he was a brahmachari and he was a very nice devotee, he was working very hard but gradually he got married, he became comfortable. When you start becoming comfortable the tendency is whatever bad habits you used to have, they become very prone to performing those activities again. So, he started become doing less and less service, he started not being very careful about following the four regulative principles, gradually he moved away from the community, he had a job, he made some nice money, but he was still a devotee. He never criticized or offended other Vaishnavas, every now and then he chanted Hare Krishna, but he was living a nice life by material standards and one day some devotees, this is many-many years ago, maybe about 10 years ago, some devotee said to this person, he said, what is it that's going to take you to become serious about Krishna consciousness again? He said I think cancer is the only thing that's gonna make me really serious about Krishna consciousness again. You know, Krishna is in your heart and He hears everything so he was telling Krishna, obviously Krishna is not concerned with anything material, He's concerned that you become serious about Krishna Consciousness again. So last year he came down with terminal cancer and he tried everything of every type of medicine to cure it nothing worked, finally they said he has two months to live. He came back to New Vrindavan and he was just hearing and chanting all day long and devotees very kindly 24 hours a day, devotees were reading the scriptures and having kirtan around him. This is happening right now. Last letter I received a few days ago devotees were having kirtan around his bed and reading and helping him chanting. He may have left by now, if not he only has a few days to go at the most. How fortunate he was. How this man is most fortunate. Now a materialist would not see it in this light. How is this man most fortunate? He is about to die and he's suffering intense pain. Generally, people think that health is the best fortune, good health, but what is his health? He cannot possibly be in worst health than this man. People say that peace of mind is good fortune but he's being racked with torturous pain and the thought of death coming at any moment. Why? Because he's taking shelter of Krishna. So, the only standard of good fortune in the eyes of the saddle is to the extent we are becoming Krishna conscious. To the extent we are determined and enthused to remember Krishna and to engage in his service. There's no other standard of good fortune. So Narada Muni and his representatives, the great devotees of the Lord, they have a vision which cannot be understood by the common man. In fact, the Bhagavad Gita explains

Qualities of a Sadhu

yā niśā sarva-bhūtānāṁ

tasyāṁ jāgarti saṁyamī

yasyāṁ jāgrati bhūtāni

sā niśā paśyato muneḥ

[Bg 2.69]

[What is night for all beings is the time of awakening for the self-controlled; and the time of awakening for all beings is night for the introspective sage.]

That what is the time of awakening for the great Saint is the time of sleeping for the ordinary man and what is the time of awakening for the ordinary man is the time of sleep for the great saints. A materialist and a spiritualist have a completely opposite perception of this world. A materialist is always very much awake to his own sense enjoyment, to his own position in society. Whereas the Sadhu is completely negligent and sleeping to these things. Whereas a great soul is very much awake to self-realization, very much awake to just trying to see the mercy of Krishna everywhere and take every opportunity with full enthusiasm to serve Krishna, the materialist is totally sleeping. Krishna consciousness is attained by our eagerness to serve. This is the price of pure devotional service, our eagerness to serve and when we see the good fortune we have, that we have the opportunity to serve we must become eager for that service.

For a devotee who has sacrificed so much of this world for the mercy of Krishna to have the opportunity to serve and to be unwilling to do that service he is most unfortunate. He's given the most and he's accepting the least. A devotee must be very-very enthusiastic and very eager for any service he can render knowing that service is the real fortune t/he real opulence of life. Under any condition devotional service is always there. There is not a single position anywhere in this material universe where you do not have the opportunity to serve your spiritual master and Krishna and that is our only fortune by remembering him, by chanting his name, by hearing about him and by offering our body, mind and words of his service, that opportunity is our only real fortune, and the great souls they see that to the degree you are enthusiastic to serve you are fortunate and to the degree you are not enthusiastic to serve we are unfortunate. Just see how people in this world they are so enthusiastic to do their jobs, just for a little position, just to please their family members, just to make a little money, they work so hard, they perform so many sacrifices but when it comes to their Krishna conscious they become very lazy they become very complacent. They think just, just my being a devotee is enough this is very unfortunate.
